rulings.Case Title: CORNELIUS MURPHY et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Fdcc California, Inc. Formerly Known As Dinwiddie Construction Company To Produce Its Custodian Of Records And Person(S) Most Qualified For Deposition, And To Produce Documents At Deposition
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, March 8, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel the deposition of FDCC California, Inc. Formerly Known As Dinwiddie Construction Company¿s custodian of records and person most qualified and request for sanctions is continued to March 29, 2011 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 220 per agreement of parties. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH GARZA et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS(B P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Compelling Answers To Second Set Of Post-Judgment Interrogatories Pursuant To C.C.P. § 708.020, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Pursuant To C.C.P. § 2030.300(D)
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, March 8, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel answers to second set of post-judgment interrogatories and request for monetary sanctions is granted. The interrogatories are reasonably calculated to lead to information to aid in enforcement of the judgment. All objections by defendant have either been previously rejected by the court or lack merit and thus defendant¿s position lacks substantial justification. Plaintiffs are awarded sanctions in the amount of $1500 (six hours at $300 per hour). There has been no showing why $625 per hour is a reasonable rate for this motion or that the motion could not have been drafted by an attorney with a lower billing rate. = (220/HEK)
